Creating Your Seed Product
Module 6
Creating your Seed Product - Overview
• Select the program or the platform for your calls
• Plan out your content
• Host the calls
• Send surveys before and between each call
• Package it up and turn it into a program you sell
How will you deliver your calls? (webinar or
teleconference)
• Webinar
• People would see slides
• Slides and a little headshot video
• Purely via video (no slides)
• Teleconference
• 100% audio
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	2
Select the program to deliver your calls
• Webinar
• GoToWebinar
• Zoom
• Google Hangouts
• Teleconference
• Freeconferencecall.com
Select the program to deliver your calls
• You don’t have to get fancy or be super amazing
• If you’re debating between webinar vs.
teleseminar - do webinar
• There’s a higher perceived value with video
Planning out your content
• Split into 5 general topic areas (can be more or
less - whatever is best for your content)
• Decide which topic makes sense to deliver first
(you can always pivot and rearrange the order as
you go)
• Create one call (outline it) at a time as you go
• Send out a survey before each call
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	3
Planning out your content
• Plan on 60 minutes of content followed by 30
minutes of follow-up questions (and modify for
your program/audience, etc.)
• Add a bonus Q&A call
• Don’t announce that until you’re half way into
the program
• (Make it an unannounced bonus)
Send surveys between each call
• Set up surveys for feedback ahead of the first call
and between each call
• You can use surveymonkey, surveygizmo, google
forms, etc.
• Keep your survey as simple and clean as possible
and if you have multiple questions, consider
splitting them up into multiple pages
• Send an email to the survey before each call
Sample call outline
• Remember… The content of the call should be
driven by the questions from your survey
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	4
Sample call outline
• Introduction (What’s going to be covered and WHY)
• Five Subtopics – then for each…
• the three most important points
• the biggest mistakes people make
• the biggest objections or obstacles people have
• Answer the top 5-10 questions people asked about
this topic
• Conclusion (Quick review, motivation, inner-game,
and encouragement)
Host the live calls
• Do a dry run on all the tech
• Practice it - maybe get a friend to listen in and
test the technical stuff
• Get familiar with capabilities of whatever hosting
platform you choose
• Create slides (if you’re going to have slides)
Host the live calls
• Practice
• Don’t obsess about practicing
• People know it’s a live call
• Record the calls
• Give homework
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

After you deliver your program…

© Internet Alchemy, Inc. All rights reserved. 	6
Package and prep your new program
• Get the recordings of the calls transcribed
(rev.com, fiverr, etc.)
• Edit/clean up transcripts
• Edit/clean up videos/audios if needed
• Create additional resources to address any gaps
• Fill in the blank templates
• Checklists, etc.
Package and prep your new program
• Create & send a post-program survey and gather
testimonials
• Create Case Studies from your star student’s
experiences / successes
• Continue to build your list and look ahead at
doing an Internal Launch ;-)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
